Tier 0 occupancy check

occupancy_normal

Day hours (07:00 – 22:00)
Tier 1 signal
aspect_ratio = 2.74  >  1.0 (standing)
Detection source
Real  YOLOv8n + aspect-ratio rule
Recommended action
No action required

In normal operation, Tier 0 runs on every frame. Frames with no people are discarded. Frames with people are checked by Tier 1. A standing aspect ratio triggers no escalation and no alert is sent to the queue.

This scenario shows two separate Tier 0 rule alerts. Both triggers are simulated; the off-hours clock and overcrowding threshold are not wired to real hardware in this demo.

Safety event: fall detected

2026-06-10 14:32:18
Room / Camera
Corridor B  /  CAM-03
Event type
safety_event_fall
Severity
HIGH
Description
Tier 1 Real: Bounding-box aspect ratio dropped from 2.31 to 0.68 over 27 frames and remained below 1.0 for 9 consecutive sampled frames. Posture consistent with person on floor.

Tier 2 Simulated: Subject appears stationary on the ground, arms at sides. Posture and lack of motion are consistent with a fall or medical event.
Trigger reason
aspect_ratio < 1.0 for 9 consecutive sampled frames; threshold 5 frames  Real signal
Evidence frame
CAM-03-14:32:06.jpg  Simulated
Evidence clip
CAM-03-14:32:03–33.mp4 (10 s buffer)  Simulated
Confidence
0.72  (Tier 1 rule alone; Tier 2 confirmation would revise this)
Recommended action
Dispatch welfare check to Corridor B immediately. Contact emergency services if no response within 2 minutes.
Detection source
Real  YOLOv8n + aspect-ratio rule    Simulated  Tier 2 VLM confirmation

Confidence 0.72 reflects the Tier 1 rule alone. Evaluation shows the rule has ~63% precision across the UR Fall dataset. Tier 2 confirmation (simulated here) would significantly improve that figure before the alert reaches the queue.

Bounding-box aspect ratio over frames: fall vs. normal sequence

Data from data-results/fall_log.csv and data-results/normal_log.csv. Every 3rd frame sampled (mirroring ~5 fps design rate). Only frames with a detected person are plotted. Real  Source: YOLOv8n on UR Fall dataset sequences fall-01-cam0 and adl-01-cam0.

What this chart shows
  • The fall sequence (red): aspect ratio starts above 2.5 (person standing), drops to 0.51–0.68 after the fall, and remains below 1.0 while the person is on the floor.
  • The normal sequence (blue): ratio ranges from 1.1 to 4.4 throughout. The person bends and crouches (ratio down to ~1.11) but never crosses below 1.0 for 5+ consecutive frames.
  • The dashed amber line at ratio = 1.0 is the fall threshold. Staying below it for 5 or more consecutive sampled frames triggers a Tier 1 candidate.
  • Evaluated on 60 UR Fall sequences (threshold ratio 1.0, persistence 5 frames, sampled every 3 frames): 12 TP, 18 FN, 7 FP, 33 TN. Precision ~63%, recall ~40%. The rule catches clear falls but misses falls that do not produce a wide bounding box: falls toward the camera, partial occlusions, slow sinks. Those 18 missed falls are why Tier 2 exists.
